claim: The reproduction prior used for BDBV detection-depth modeling is not directly grounded in a BDBV-specific basic reproduction number estimate.
value: Exact internal parameterization omitted from the public audit extract.
source_refs: Wamala JF, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010; 16(7):1087-1092. DOI 10.3201/eid1607.091525.; Van Kerkhove MD, et al. Scientific Data 2015;2:150019. DOI 10.1038/sdata.2015.19.; Lekone PE, Finkenstadt BF. Biometrics 2006;62(4):1170-1177. DOI 10.1111/j.1541-0420.2006.00609.x.
public_action: Do not cite the reproduction prior as BDBV-specific R0 evidence until a direct source or explicit derivation is added.

claim: The reporting-completeness interval uses a peer-reviewed onset-to-notification delay estimate as a cross-species proxy, with the species-transfer limitation disclosed.
value: Camacho 2015 onset-to-notification proxy; implementation constants omitted from the public audit extract.
source_refs: Camacho A, et al. PLOS Currents Outbreaks 2015. DOI 10.1371/currents.outbreaks.406ae55e83ec0b5193e30856b9235ed2.; Cori A, et al. American Journal of Epidemiology 2013;178(9):1505-1512. DOI 10.1093/aje/kwt133.; WHO Ebola Response Team. New England Journal of Medicine 2014;371:1481-1495. DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa1411100.
public_action: Keep the reporting-completeness result labeled as a cross-species proxy unless BDBV-specific reporting-delay data are added.
public_note: Internal audit IDs and review locators are withheld from this public export.

claim: The BDBV prior-outbreak CFR scenario band is 26/33/40 percent (central 33), per the US CDC 55/169 aggregate and the Imperial 20 May 2026 update. It supersedes the 18 May 24/30/40 set and the older unsupported 30-50 percent range.
source_refs: Wamala JF, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010;16(7):1087-1092. DOI 10.3201/eid1607.091525.; US CDC. History of Ebola Outbreaks.; Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the BDBV outbreak in DRC: 20 May 2026 update.; Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in DRC. 18 May 2026 (superseded).
public_action: Keep the CFR band linked to this audit row at 26/33/40 (central 33). Re-sync the website snapshot's methodology_constants so the live InferredTrajectory band reflects 26/40. Avoid reintroducing the 24/30/40 set or the unsupported 30-50 percent range.
public_note: Internal audit IDs and review locators are withheld from this public export.

claim: The WA 2014 retrospective skill scores are computed from a Backer-Wallinga 62-prefecture, 74-week substrate; the reported W3-W11 evaluation window is a local deterministic protocol, not a source-imposed capture range.
value: W3, W5, W7, W9, W11 as-of weeks; 4-week horizon; no-context Brier 0.0586, WIS 0.1002, ECE 0.0391
source_refs: Backer JA, Wallinga J. PLOS Computational Biology 2016;12(12):e1005210. DOI 10.1371/journal.pcbi.1005210.; data/west-africa-prefecture-weekly.json metadata, derived from Backer-Wallinga S1 Data.; lovs/lovs_validation.py deterministic Mode A backtest.
public_action: Keep the W3-W11 window described as a local validation protocol; cite Backer-Wallinga only for the substrate, not for the window choice.
public_note: Internal audit IDs and review locators are withheld from this public export.

claim: Imperial Table 3's Nord Kivu summary total can be adjudicated against the underlying WHO epiweek 10/11/15/18 sitreps.
value: Imperial's published Nord Kivu PoE summary total does not reconcile with the sum of its own Table 3 per-PoE rows (off by 18 daily travellers); absolute counts are restricted and not redistributed here
source_refs: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in DRC. 18 May 2026.; WHO AFRO Weekly External Situation Report 01, data as of 18 May 2026.; Restricted local review notes for Imperial Table 3; not redistributed in the public repository.
public_action: Do not redistribute the extracted Table 3 PoE dataset. Reopen only if WHO publishes the underlying epiweek 10/11/15/18 PoE passenger-count sitreps, Imperial grants permission, or Imperial issues an erratum.
public_note: Internal audit IDs and review locators are withheld from this public export.

claim: The May 2026 BDBV staged count observations preserve separate confirmed, probable, suspected, suspected-death, and approximate-media denominators instead of treating CDC, WHO speech, ECDC, and aggregator values as interchangeable.
